2603.29997.title: Enhancing Structural Mapping with LLM-derived Abstractions for Analogical Reasoning in Narratives
2603.29997.paper_summary: Analogical reasoning is a key driver of human generalization in problem-solving and argumentation. Yet, analogies between narrative structures remain challenging for machines. Cognitive engines for structural mapping are not directly applicable, as they assume pre-extracted entities, whereas LLMs' performance is sensitive to prompt format and the degree of surface similarity between narratives. This gap motivates a key question: What is the impact of enhancing structural mapping with LLM-derived abstractions on their analogical reasoning ability in narratives? To that end, we propose a modular framework named YARN (Yielding Abstractions for Reasoning in Narratives), which uses LLMs to decompose narratives into units, abstract these units, and then passes them to a mapping component that aligns elements across stories to perform analogical reasoning. We define and operationalize four levels of abstraction that capture both the general meaning of units and their roles in the story, grounded in prior work on framing. Our experiments reveal that abstractions consistently improve model performance, resulting in competitive or better performance than end-to-end LLM baselines. Closer error analysis reveals the remaining challenges in abstraction at the right level, in incorporating implicit causality, and an emerging categorization of analogical patterns in narratives. YARN enables systematic variation of experimental settings to analyze component contributions, and to support future work, we make the code for YARN openly available.
2603.29997.paper_summary_zh: 類比推理是人類在解決問題和論證中進行概括的關鍵驅動力。<lb/>然而，敘事結構之間的類比對於機器來說仍然具有挑戰性。<lb/>結構映射的認知引擎並不直接適用，因為它們假設已經提取的實體，而大型語言模型（LLMs）的性能對提示格式和敘事之間的表面相似度的程度非常敏感。<lb/>這一差距引發了一個關鍵問題：增強結構映射與LLM衍生抽象對敘事中的類比推理能力有何影響？<lb/>為此，我們提出了一個名為YARN（Yielding Abstractions for Reasoning in Narratives）的模組化框架，該框架使用LLMs將敘事分解為單元，抽象這些單元，然後將它們傳遞給一個映射組件，該組件對齊故事中的元素以進行類比推理。<lb/>我們定義並實現了四個層次的抽象，這些層次既捕捉單元的一般意義，又捕捉它們在故事中的角色，這基於先前的框架研究。<lb/>我們的實驗顯示，抽象始終改善模型性能，導致與端到端LLM基準相當或更好的性能。<lb/>更深入的錯誤分析揭示了在正確層次進行抽象、納入隱含因果關係以及在敘事中出現的類比模式分類方面的剩餘挑戰。<lb/>YARN使實驗設置的系統變化成為可能，以分析組件貢獻，並為支持未來的工作，我們將YARN的代碼公開提供。
2603.29997.author: Mohammadhossein Khojasteh et.al.
2603.29997.authors: Mohammadhossein Khojasteh, Yifan Jiang, Stefano De Giorgis, Frank van Harmelen, Filip Ilievski
